title: calceolaria oreophila
intro: Calceolaria oreophila, also known as the "Pocket Flower," is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Calceolaria genus, native to the Andes in South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calceolaria oreophila include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
